content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Apr 28, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development activity on April 28, 2026, focused on dependency management and infrastructure maintenance. A stack of pull requests has been opened to address pending updates identified by the Dependency Dashboard, with efforts currently gated by a required fix for a broken `bun install` process caused by a missing package version.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Dependency Updates and Infrastructure**
*   A stack of PRs has been opened to update core dependencies, including Node.js to v24, TypeScript to v6, Rollup to 4.60.2, Rimraf to v6, and @capacitor/core to 8.3.1.
*   Progress is currently blocked by the need to merge a prerequisite fix ([#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146)) to resolve a transitive dependency issue with `@elizaos/plugin-rolodex`.

**CI/CD and Workflow Observations**
*   Identified that the main `ci.yaml` workflow has been inactive since February.
*   Noted that current CI workflows fail to invoke `bun install` or `tsc`, allowing dependency breakages and typecheck failures to persist undetected.
*   Identified a path error in the `generate:types` script where it attempts to access a non-existent directory.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   [#79](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/79) Dependency Dashboard: 
    *   The dashboard is currently tracking a stack of PRs ([#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146), [#7147](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7147), [#7148](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7148), [#7149](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7149), [#7150](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7150), [#7151](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7151)) aimed at updating the project's dependency stack.
    *   Development is gated by [#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146) to resolve a `bun install` failure.
    *   The TypeScript v6 update ([#7151](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7151)) has surfaced a pre-existing typecheck failure in `@elizaos/autonomous` that requires a follow-up fix.
